title: catha integrifolia
intro: Catha integrifolia, also known as khat, is a flowering plant native to the Horn of Africa and the Arabian Peninsula, belonging to the Celastraceae family. The leaves of the Catha integrifolia plant contain cathinone, a stimulant that has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, including reducing fatigue and increasing alertness. The health benefits of Catha integrifolia include improved mental clarity, increased energy, and reduced symptoms of depression and anxiety, although its long-term use has been associated with negative effects. Catha integrifolia is typically consumed as a herbal tea, chewed fresh, or smoked, but its use is heavily regulated in many countries due to concerns over addiction and other health risks.
